profile.name: Lorenzo Rizzo
profile.title: Data Analyst
profile.bio:
Studente di Data Science al Politecnico di Torino con una base in Fisica e competenze pratiche in SQL, Python e Tableau. Ho 
sviluppato Domina, una piattaforma di market intelligence immobiliare, seguendo l'intero ciclo dalla customer discovery al 
deployment. Cerco un ruolo da Data Analyst dove trasformare dati complessi in insight azionabili.

projects.title: Customer Churn Prediction
projects.shortDescription: Binary classification model identifying at-risk customers 30 days in advance.
projects.fullDescription:
This project addressed a critical business problem: identifying customers likely to churn before they cancel their subscription. I built a full ML pipeline using historical transactional data across 18 months.

The methodology involved feature engineering from raw event logs, handling severe class imbalance via SMOTE, and comparing Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost. The final model used an ensemble approach with calibrated probabilities.

Deployment was handled as a weekly batch job feeding results into a CRM dashboard, enabling the retention team to prioritize outreach.
results: AUC-ROC of 0.91 on held-out test set
Reduced churn by 18% in the first quarter post-deployment
Processed 2.3M customer records per batch run

projects.title: Executive Sales Dashboard
projects.shortDescription: Real-time Power BI dashboard consolidating five data sources into one view.
projects.fullDescription:
The sales leadership team was working from five disconnected spreadsheets updated manually each Monday. This project consolidated those sources — Salesforce, ERP exports, a Google Sheet forecast model, and two internal databases — into a single live Power BI report.

The data pipeline was built in Python using scheduled Azure Data Factory runs. Data modeling followed a star-schema approach with a central fact table and conformed dimensions for region, product, and time.

I conducted three workshops with stakeholders to validate KPI definitions before building, which significantly reduced revision cycles.
results: Reduced weekly reporting preparation time from 6 hours to 20 minutes
Adopted by 40+ users across 3 regional offices
Identified a €280K revenue leakage previously hidden in data silos

projects.title: Product Review Sentiment Analysis
projects.shortDescription: NLP pipeline extracting feature-level sentiment from 50K+ e-commerce reviews.
projects.fullDescription:
Rather than a simple positive/negative classifier, this project aimed to extract granular, aspect-level sentiment — understanding not just that a review is negative, but which product attribute (delivery speed, packaging, quality) is causing dissatisfaction.

I used a fine-tuned BERT model on a domain-specific labeled dataset of 8,000 reviews, combined with a rule-based aspect extraction layer. Results were stored in a PostgreSQL database and visualized in a Streamlit app.

The product team used findings to prioritize the Q3 packaging redesign, which later contributed to a 0.4-star average rating improvement.
results: Aspect-level F1 score of 0.84 across 6 product categories
Directly influenced Q3 product roadmap decisions
